questions.question: Match each data type to the train/test split that fits it.
categories: Chronological split (and time-series CV)
Group split by subject ID
Stratified split
questions.explanation: Chronological splits stop the future leaking into the past, group splits keep one subject on one side to stop subject-level leakage, and stratified splits preserve class proportions on both sides under imbalance.

questions.question: Match each leakage pitfall to its correct fix.
items: Scaling on the full dataset before splitting
Target derived from features kept as inputs
categories: Fit the scaler on training only, apply to test
Run SMOTE inside each fold
Drop the source variables from the inputs
Use a group split by participant ID

correct_mapping.Scaling on the full dataset before split: Fit the scaler on training only, apply to test
correct_mapping.Target derived from features kept as inp: Drop the source variables from the inputs
correct_mapping.Random row-split with repeated subjects: Use a group split by participant ID

questions.explanation: Each fix keeps test information out of the fitted model: scalers fit on train only, SMOTE runs per fold, target-source variables are removed, and a group split isolates each subject.

questions.question: Match each evaluation metric or baseline to its purpose under imbalance.
categories: Balances precision and recall in one number
Fraction of predicted positives that are correct
Fraction of true positives that are detected
The accuracy a trivial majority predictor would reach

correct_mapping.F1 / F-beta score: Balances precision and recall in one number
correct_mapping.Precision: Fraction of predicted positives that are correct
correct_mapping.Recall (sensitivity): Fraction of true positives that are detected
correct_mapping.Majority-class baseline: The accuracy a trivial majority predictor would reach

questions.explanation: On imbalanced data these metrics expose minority-class performance, and the majority-class baseline is the bar a model must beat, not the 50% chance level.

questions.question: Match each ML figure to what it shows.
categories: Counts of TP, FP, FN, TN for each class
Trade-off above a dashed chance diagonal, summarized by AUC
Ranked contribution of the top features

correct_mapping.Confusion matrix: Counts of TP, FP, FN, TN for each class
correct_mapping.ROC curve: Trade-off above a dashed chance diagonal, summarized by AUC
correct_mapping.Feature-importance plot: Ranked contribution of the top features

questions.explanation: A confusion matrix breaks down TP/FP/FN/TN (where many true negatives can inflate accuracy under imbalance), the ROC curve should sit above its dashed chance diagonal, and feature-importance plots show only the top features for readability.

questions.question: Match each transparency or analysis term to its definition.
categories: Presenting an exploratory result as a planned hypothesis
Reporting only the analyses that reached significance
Specifying confirmatory analyses before data collection
Data touched once for the final unbiased estimate

correct_mapping.HARKing: Presenting an exploratory result as a planned hypothesis
correct_mapping.P-hacking: Reporting only the analyses that reached significance
correct_mapping.Pre-registration: Specifying confirmatory analyses before data collection
correct_mapping.Held-out test set: Data touched once for the final unbiased estimate

questions.explanation: HARKing and p-hacking are the transparency violations, while pre-registration and a locked held-out test set are protections for confirmatory work and for honest predictive estimates.

questions.question: Match each analysis flavour or model to its description.
categories: Tests a causal hypothesis; risks p-hacking and HARKing
Makes accurate predictions; risks overfitting and leakage
Unifies t-test, ANOVA, and regression as y = X-beta + epsilon
Adds random effects for nested data as y = X-beta + Z-u + epsilon

correct_mapping.Explanatory / behavioural analysis: Tests a causal hypothesis; risks p-hacking and HARKing
correct_mapping.Predictive / ML analysis: Makes accurate predictions; risks overfitting and leakage
correct_mapping.General linear model: Unifies t-test, ANOVA, and regression as y = X-beta + epsilon
correct_mapping.Linear mixed model: Adds random effects for nested data as y = X-beta + Z-u + epsilon

questions.explanation: Explanatory work tests hypotheses and predictive work optimizes accuracy; the general linear model underlies the common behavioural tests, while the linear mixed model extends it with random effects for repeated-measures or nested data.
